prevalence.source: European aging cohort syntheses / WHO Europe context
prevalence.trend: increasing with aging demographics and improved screening

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Cognitive concern with objective impairment and preserved functional independence; cognitive testing (MoCA/MMSE) and reversible-cause workup; memory clinic pathways common

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; opportunistic screening and memory clinic referral pathways in many countries
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; risk-factor control and cognitive interventions more accessible in many member states

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Memory clinics and structured referral pathways common; access varies by member state
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Vascular risk control, cognitive stimulation, physical activity, hearing interventions, and medication review
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Service demand and access variability across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong primary care infrastructure and specialist pathways for cognitive assessment in many systems
